calendar.matters:
Ntoice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-19-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Prior to the hearing, Mr. Punchin appears via Zoom. At the instruction of the Court, the clerk informs Mr. Ounchin the matter will not be heard for failure to comply with the tentative ruling rule and the Court's TR will be the final ruling of the Court. Mr. Punchin to check the court's website re:2 proposed orders submitted.

No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - Defendant Paul Punchin's unopposed Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment is OFF CALENDAR.

On February 4, 2026, the court issued a tentative ruling and ordered Defendant to "prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing," which, of course, was February 5, 2026, at 9:00 am. Defendant failed to comply with the court's order. And now more than six weeks later, the court still has no proposed order memorializing the 2/4/2026 tentative ruling, which was adopted on 2/5/2026. The court declines to rule on any motion filed by Defendant as long as Defendant is in violation of the 2/4/2026 directive and order.

The court, again, orders Defendant to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the 2/4/2026 tentative ruling and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for the 3/19/2026 hearing.

Additionally, the court orders Defendant to prepare a proposed order which repeats the above text verbatim and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for the 3/19/2026 hearing.

The Court signs the order for today's hearing. the Court rejects the proposed order submitted for the 3/5/36 hearing since it is not verbatim. Mr. Punchin to resubmit a proposed order for the hearing held on 2/5/26 and must drop a hard copy in Dept. 302.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-18-2026 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PAUL PUNCHIN Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-05-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Paul Punchin's unopposed Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment is OFF CALENDAR.

Moving Defendant's proof of service is unsubscribed and, thus, of no evidentiary value. (See Code of Civil Procedure sections 1013a and 2015.5.) Thus, Moving Defendant did not timely demonstrate that he served the moving papers. (See California Rules of Court, rule 3.1300(c).) Additionally, Moving Defendant did not timely serve their moving papers. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b) [motion must be filed and served 16 court days before hearing]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(a) [service by mail where place of address or place of mailing is outside State of California but within United Stated adds ten calendar days to "any period of notice"].) Also, Compton declares on 1/1/2026 that he is in Los Angeles and that mailed the papers from San Francisco on the same day. The court is not convinced. Finally, none of Defendant's papers are properly subscribed. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 128.7(a).)

Moving Defendant must prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

No proposed order received. Plaintiff failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-31-2023 Off Calendar
